---

## 🐬 About

**HackNlearn India Firmware** is a custom Flipper Zero firmware UI built for the Indian hacking and security research community. This project is designed for **educational purposes** — to learn about wireless protocols, hardware hacking, and embedded security research.

---

## ✨ Features

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| 📡 **Sub-GHz** | Scan, record, and replay Sub-GHz signals (433MHz, 315MHz, 868MHz) |
| 💳 **NFC / RFID** | Read, write, and emulate NFC cards (MIFARE, EMV, ISO14443A/B) |
| ⌨️ **BadUSB** | HID payload scripts for security testing |
| 🔴 **IR Blaster** | Universal IR remote with 2800+ device database |
| 🔌 **GPIO** | Full GPIO control, UART, SPI, I2C interface |
| 🔵 **BLE Tools** | Bluetooth LE scanning, device enumeration |

---

## 🚀 Getting Started

### Web UI Preview
Open `index.html` in any modern browser to see the firmware UI demo.

### Live Demo
> 🌐 [hacknlearnindia.github.io/flipper-firmware](https://hacknlearnindia1.github.io/HackNlearn-Flipper/)

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
hacknlearn-flipper/
├── index.html              ← Main firmware UI (web demo)
├── README.md               ← This file
├── LICENSE                 ← MIT License
├── src/
│   ├── main.c              ← Core firmware entry (coming soon)
│   ├── subghz.c            ← Sub-GHz module
│   ├── nfc.c               ← NFC/RFID module
│   └── badusb.c            ← BadUSB engine
├── payloads/
│   ├── badusb/
│   │   ├── whoami.txt      ← Sample BadUSB script
│   │   └── net_scan.txt    ← Network scan payload
│   ├── subghz/             ← Sub-GHz signal captures
│   └── ir/                 ← IR remote databases
└── assets/
    └── screenshots/        ← UI screenshots
```

---

## ⚠️ Legal Disclaimer

This firmware and all tools in this repository are intended **strictly for educational and research purposes**. Always:

- Get **explicit permission** before testing on any device or network
- Follow all **local laws and regulations**
- Use only on devices **you own or have permission to test**

The HackNlearn India team is **not responsible** for any misuse of this software.

## 🤝 Contributing

Pull requests welcome! If you want to contribute:

1. Fork the repo
2. Create your feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`)
3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature'`)
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`)
5. Open a Pull Request
